incidents.victim: IoT device owners and DDoS targets worldwide
incidents.vector: Exploited vulnerable IoT devices (routers, cameras, DVRs, Android TV boxes) and abused residential proxy networks to build four botnets — Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad.
incidents.impact: Three million devices hijacked; over 300,000 DDoS attacks launched including a record 29.7 Tbps attack targeting U.S. Department of Defense networks. DOJ seized infrastructure with support from Canada, Germany, the Netherlands, and Europol.

incidents.vector: Data analyst contractor used company laptop access to exfiltrate corporate data including employee PII and compensation records, then sent 60+ extortion emails demanding ransom after his contract ended.
incidents.impact: Company paid approximately $2.5 million in ransom. Attacker convicted on six counts of extortion and faces up to 12 years in prison.

incidents.vector: Compromised Microsoft Intune endpoint management console and remotely triggered mass device wipes across the company's global network — no malware deployed.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 devices wiped across 79 countries; 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland; personal employee devices with Intune also wiped. DOJ seized four MOIS-linked domains; FBI and CISA issued joint Intune hardening advisory. Restoration underway but incomplete.

incidents.vector: Phishing email exploiting a cross-site scripting flaw in Zimbra webmail (CVE-2025-66376) — entire attack payload embedded in the HTML body of a single email with no attachments or links.
incidents.impact: Attackers harvested login credentials, session tokens, two-factor authentication backup codes, browser-stored passwords, and up to 90 days of mailbox data from the maritime navigation agency.

incidents.victim: European organizations across six EU member states
incidents.attacker: China — state-linked cyber contractors; Iran — MOIS
incidents.vector: EU sanctioned three entities: Integrity Technology Group compromised 65,000 devices; i-SOON conducted hack-for-hire operations for Chinese military/intelligence; Emennet Pasargad hacked Swedish SMS service and ran Paris Olympics disinformation.
incidents.impact: Sanctions freeze EU assets and impose travel bans. Reveals scale of state-sponsored operations against European targets. All three entities were previously sanctioned by the US and UK.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to a warehouse management system used for Thai procurement parts logistics.
incidents.impact: Employee PII from Mazda group companies and suppliers potentially leaked externally. No customer data was affected. Mazda warned of potential phishing using the stolen email addresses.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian website visitors and iPhone users globally
incidents.attacker: Unknown — multiple state and commercial spyware operators
incidents.vector: Watering hole attack embedding the 'DarkSword' iOS exploit kit on dozens of Ukrainian websites, targeting iPhones running iOS 18.4 through 18.6.2.
incidents.impact: Second iOS exploit kit discovered this month. Capable of stealing data and crypto wallet information. An estimated 220–270 million iPhones remain on vulnerable iOS versions. Separate campaigns confirmed by Google across Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.

incidents.victim: Bitrefill (crypto gift card and SIM platform)
incidents.vector: Compromised an employee laptop to gain initial access, then moved laterally through internal systems.
incidents.impact: Cryptocurrency assets stolen, customer records exfiltrated, and gift card inventory tampering attempted. Company attributed the breach to North Korean state-sponsored operators.

incidents.victim: P3 Global Intel / Navigate360 (law enforcement tip platform)
incidents.vector: Exploited a vulnerability in the P3 Global Intel tip submission platform used by police departments across the United States.
incidents.impact: 93 GB of confidential law enforcement tips leaked to journalists and DDoSecrets. Unencrypted data could deanonymize anonymous tipsters, posing safety risks to informants.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack crippled the company's IT network and cloud connectivity for ignition interlock devices.
incidents.impact: DUI-convicted drivers unable to start their vehicles for over two days as breathalyzer devices could not connect to company servers for verification.

incidents.vector: Flash loan exploit manipulating protocol logic to drain funds — the platform's second major hack after a $27 million theft in September.
incidents.impact: $3.6 million in cryptocurrency stolen. Highlights persistent smart contract vulnerabilities in DeFi protocols.

incidents.vector: Three unprotected databases containing AI chatbot interaction data left publicly accessible on the internet without authentication.
incidents.impact: 3.7 million customer chat logs, audio recordings, and text transcripts from 2024–2026 exposed. Data included customer support interactions with personal details.

incidents.victim: Trust Wallet users and other cryptocurrency holders
incidents.vector: Supply chain intrusions via compromised Electron apps and browser plugins that collected private keys and seed phrases. Operated through a shell security firm (Wuhan Anshun) exposed by a disgruntled employee.
incidents.impact: Trust Wallet users lost $7 million in December alone. Additional victims across multiple cryptocurrency platforms. Operation linked to a Chinese entity conducting systematic crypto theft at scale.

incidents.vector: Interlock ransomware gang exploited a zero-day deserialization flaw in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (CVE-2026-20131, CVSS 10) weeks before public disclosure to gain initial access.
incidents.impact: Multiple critical infrastructure organizations compromised via double-extortion (encryption plus data theft). Past victims include St. Paul, MN city government (National Guard deployed), DaVita dialysis, Ohio healthcare system, and K-12 schools. Amazon discovered the full operational toolkit via a misconfigured Interlock staging server.
